title: Foundation and Core Integrations
impact: High - Establishes common types and integrates TaskScheduler with core managers

progressCallback.current: Custom or none in most managers
progressCallback.target: Unified ProgressCallback type (from taskScheduler.ts) used by all long-running operations

cancellation.target: AbortSignal support in EntityManager, CompressionManager, IOManager

backwardCompatibility.target: All existing method signatures remain unchanged; options parameter is optional

tasks.title: Create Common Types and Operation Utilities
tasks.description: Create shared types and utility functions for long-running operations with progress tracking and cancellation support. Uses the existing ProgressCallback from taskScheduler.ts.

implementation.purpose: Provide consistent type definitions and utilities for async operations, enabling uniform progress reporting and cancellation patterns.
keyDecisions: Use existing ProgressCallback from taskScheduler.ts (object-based: {completed, total, percentage, currentTaskId?})
Create LongRunningOperationOptions interface with onProgress, signal, and priority
Create OperationCancelledError class for cancellation errors
Create utility functions in operationUtils.ts (checkCancellation, createProgressReporter, createProgress, executeWithPhases)
All parameters are optional to maintain backward compatibility

stepByStep.details: Import ProgressCallback and TaskPriority from taskScheduler. Create interface with onProgress?: ProgressCallback, signal?: AbortSignal, priority?: TaskPriority
Extend KnowledgeGraphError with 'OPERATION_CANCELLED' code. Include optional operation name in message.
Implement checkCancellation(), createProgressReporter() with throttling, createProgress(), and executeWithPhases()
Add exports for all operationUtils functions and OperationCancelledError
npm run typecheck

acceptanceCriteria: operationUtils.ts created with all utility functions
No breaking changes to existing code

tasks.title: Enhance EntityManager.createEntities() with Progress and Cancellation
tasks.description: Add optional progress tracking and cancellation support to the createEntities method while maintaining full backward compatibility. Method signature adds optional parameter only.

implementation.purpose: Enable progress tracking and cancellation during bulk entity creation, improving UX for large imports.
keyDecisions: Add optional LongRunningOperationOptions parameter to createEntities
Keep original return type Promise<Entity[]> (NOT CancellableResult wrapper)
Report progress after each batch of entities (using throttled reporter)
Support AbortSignal for cancellation mid-operation (throws OperationCancelledError)
Maintain backward compatibility - existing calls work unchanged
implementation.newSignature: async createEntities(entities: Entity[], options?: LongRunningOperationOptions): Promise<Entity[]>

stepByStep.details: Import checkCancellation, createProgressReporter, createProgress from utils/index.js and LongRunningOperationOptions from types/index.js
Add optional options parameter: async createEntities(entities: Entity[], options?: LongRunningOperationOptions): Promise<Entity[]>

stepByStep.action: Add progress and cancellation logic at method start
stepByStep.details: Call checkCancellation, create throttled progress reporter, report initial progress (0%)

stepByStep.action: Add progress updates during entity processing
stepByStep.details: Report progress every 100 entities, check cancellation periodically
Call reportProgress with 100% before return

stepByStep.action: Run existing tests to verify backward compatibility
stepByStep.details: npx vitest run tests/unit/core/EntityManager.test.ts

stepByStep.action: Add new tests for progress and cancellation
stepByStep.details: Test progress callback receives updates, test cancellation throws OperationCancelledError

acceptanceCriteria: Progress callback receives updates during operation (using ProgressCallback type)
All existing tests pass WITHOUT modification (backward compatible)

tasks.title: Enhance CompressionManager with Progress and Cancellation
tasks.description: Add progress tracking and cancellation support to findDuplicates() and compressGraph() methods. These are O(n²) operations that benefit most from progress tracking.

implementation.purpose: Enable progress tracking during duplicate detection and graph compression, which can be slow for large graphs.
keyDecisions: Add optional LongRunningOperationOptions to both methods
Keep original return types (NOT CancellableResult wrappers)
findDuplicates reports progress per type bucket processed
compressGraph reports two-phase progress: detection (0-40%) + merging (40-100%)

currentCode.compressGraph: async compressGraph(threshold: number = DEFAULT_DUPLICATE_THRESHOLD, dryRun: boolean = false): Promise<CompressionResult>

newSignatures.findDuplicates: async findDuplicates(threshold?: number, options?: LongRunningOperationOptions): Promise<string[][]>
newSignatures.compressGraph: async compressGraph(threshold?: number, dryRun?: boolean, options?: LongRunningOperationOptions): Promise<CompressionResult>

stepByStep.details: Import checkCancellation, createProgressReporter, createProgress from utils/index.js
Add optional options parameter after threshold
Count type buckets, report progress per bucket, check cancellation between buckets
Add optional options parameter after dryRun
Phase 1 (findDuplicates): 0-40%. Phase 2 (merging): 40-100%. Pass scaled progress to findDuplicates.

stepByStep.action: Run tests and add new test cases
stepByStep.details: Verify backward compatibility, add progress/cancellation tests

acceptanceCriteria: Progress callbacks report meaningful phase information

tasks.title: Enhance IOManager.importGraph() with Progress and Cancellation
tasks.description: Add progress tracking and cancellation support to the importGraph method with phased progress reporting for parsing and merging phases.

implementation.purpose: Enable progress tracking during graph imports, which can be slow for large datasets.
keyDecisions: Keep original return type ImportResult (NOT CancellableResult)
Report three-phase progress: parsing (0-20%), entity processing (20-70%), relation processing (70-100%)
Support cancellation after parsing and between entity/relation batches

currentCode.signature: async importGraph(format: ImportFormat, data: string, mergeStrategy: MergeStrategy = 'skip', dryRun: boolean = false): Promise<ImportResult>

implementation.newSignature: async importGraph(format: ImportFormat, data: string, mergeStrategy?: MergeStrategy, dryRun?: boolean, options?: LongRunningOperationOptions): Promise<ImportResult>

stepByStep.details: Import checkCancellation, createProgressReporter, createProgress from utils/index.js
Add optional options parameter at the end
Report progress after successful parsing, check cancellation

stepByStep.action: Add Phase 2 progress for entity processing (20-70%)
stepByStep.details: Report progress every 100 entities during merge loop

stepByStep.action: Add Phase 3 progress for relation processing (70-100%)
stepByStep.details: Report progress every 100 relations during merge loop

stepByStep.action: Run tests to verify backward compatibility
stepByStep.details: npx vitest run tests/unit/features/ImportManager.test.ts

acceptanceCriteria: Progress reports parsing, entity, and relation processing phases

successCriteria: All four tasks completed with passing tests
Common operation types established in types.ts
EntityManager, CompressionManager, and IOManager support progress callbacks
All three managers support AbortSignal cancellation
Backward compatibility maintained for all existing API calls
npm test passes with all new tests
notes: CRITICAL: Use existing ProgressCallback from taskScheduler.ts - DO NOT create new type
Backward compatibility is essential - all existing tests must pass without modification
Methods keep original return types - NO CancellableResult<T> wrappers
Progress callbacks use object format: { completed, total, percentage, currentTaskId? }
Test with large datasets (1000+ entities) to verify progress/cancellation works
